summary: Iteration 198: Code review verified all export formatting changes for consistent PDF/Word/Print output across BarristerView, ReportView, and CaseDetail. Unable to complete full UI testing due to Google OAuth authentication requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: exportHtml.js (Lines 62-75) contains CSS classes for .section, .section-header, .section-number, .section-title, .section-body with correct styling: section-header has flex display + 4px left border, section-number is circular badge (26px), section-title is Times New Roman 14pt bold uppercase, section-body has bordered container

VERIFIED: CaseDetail.jsx buildProgressHtml (Lines 915-950) uses section-header pattern with numbered badges for Case Summary, Case Readiness Score, Pipeline Summary, AI Progress Analysis, Appeal Checklist sections

VERIFIED: CaseDetail.jsx buildPrintAllHtml (Lines 987-996) has dark coloured metadata header (#0f172a background) with DEFENDANT, OFFENCE, SENTENCE, DOCUMENTS, TIMELINE EVENTS in grid layout with white text
VERIFIED: CaseDetail.jsx buildPrintAllHtml (Lines 1009-1011) has TOC with 'CONTENTS (X SECTIONS)' header in 2-column grid with numbered bold uppercase items

VERIFIED: CaseDetail.jsx buildPrintAllHtml (Lines 1018-1113) uses section-header pattern (not plain h2 tags) for all sections: Case Summary, Uploaded Documents, Timeline Events, Grounds of Merit, Notes, Progress Analysis
VERIFIED: BarristerView.jsx (Lines 527-529) has CSS for section-header, section-number, section-title with correct styling
VERIFIED: BarristerView.jsx (Lines 423-425) has TOC with 'CONTENTS (X SECTIONS)' header in 2-column grid with numbered bold uppercase items
VERIFIED: BarristerView.jsx (Lines 646-672) uses section-header pattern with numbered badges for all sections
VERIFIED: ReportView.jsx (Lines 805-807) has CSS for section-header, section-number, section-title with correct styling
VERIFIED: ReportView.jsx (Line 924) has TOC with 'CONTENTS (X SECTIONS)' header in 2-column grid with numbered bold uppercase items
VERIFIED: ReportView.jsx (Lines 928-930) uses section-header pattern with numbered badges for all sections
VERIFIED: CaseDetail.jsx header (Lines 1204-1226) contains Print All, PDF All, Word All buttons with correct data-testid attributes

success_rate.frontend: Code review 100% - UI testing blocked by Google OAuth

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 198 verified via code review that all 4 export locations (BarristerView, ReportView, CaseDetail buildProgressHtml, CaseDetail buildPrintAllHtml) use consistent formatting: (1) Coloured metadata header with DEFENDANT, OFFENCE, SENTENCE, DOCUMENTS, TIMELINE EVENTS in grid, (2) TOC with 'CONTENTS (X SECTIONS)' header in 2-column grid with numbered bold uppercase items, (3) Section headers with coloured left border, numbered circle badge, and bold uppercase title, (4) Body text in bordered containers, (5) Disclaimer + branding footer. The app uses Emergent Google OAuth which cannot be automated - manual testing or authenticated session required for full UI verification.

details.section_header_style: display:flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; border-left:4px solid ${accentColor}; padding-left:12px
details.section_title_style: font-family:'Times New Roman'; font-size:14pt; font-weight:700; text-transform:uppercase

details.toc_format: CONTENTS (X SECTIONS) with 2-column grid, numbered bold uppercase items
details.section_pattern: section-header with section-number badge and section-title
